title: Codonoboea stolonifera
intro: Codonoboea stolonifera, commonly known as the creeping Codonoboea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and modulating immune responses. The bioactive constituents of Codonoboea stolonifera include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and infusions.

Members of Gesneriaceae are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. They are herbaceous plants, often with showy flowers, and typically thrive in shaded and humid environments.
